questions.original_question: What is the difference between different sales center?
questions.answer:
Lotus Technology operates several types of sales centers, each designed to cater to different aspects of the customer experience. The **ZEEKR Center** serves as a high-end showroom located in urban commercial centers, offering a space for customer interaction, community events, and brand engagement. These centers typically occupy 300 to 600 square meters and are key touchpoints for building brand reputation and understanding customer needs. As of December 31, 2023, there were 24 ZEEKR Centers in China and two overseas.

The **ZEEKR Space** is a more flexible and smaller-scale retail space, usually ranging from 100 to 300 square meters, located in commercial areas. These spaces allow customers to explore products and technologies while participating in various events. Lotus Technology has also introduced **Pop-Up ZEEKR Spaces** in China, which offer even more flexible leasing terms to connect with a broader customer base.

For product delivery, Lotus Technology utilizes **ZEEKR Delivery Centers**, which are typically located in larger facilities outside urban centers to accommodate vehicle storage and simultaneous deliveries. As of December 31, 2023, there were 31 such centers in China.

Additionally, **ZEEKR Houses** are established in areas where car dealerships are located across different tiers of cities in China. These centers leverage the cluster effect to provide customers with a superior one-stop experience, including vehicle purchase, charging solutions, and financial services. As of December 31, 2023, there were 45 ZEEKR Houses in China.

Each type of sales center plays a distinct role in Lotus Technology’s customer-oriented direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales model, ensuring a comprehensive and engaging experience for customers at every stage of their journey.

The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

title: Direct Sales and Service Model 
summary: ZEEKR adopts a customer-centric, go-to-market strategy, spearheaded by a proficient in-house sales and marketing team that oversees its direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales network. This network is designed to provide a superior customer experience throughout the entire engagement lifecycle, focusing on site selection, construction, and operation of sales centers, as well as comprehensive delivery and after-sales services. As of December 31, 2023, approximately 84.8% of the sales and marketing team members have significant experience in the automobile and retail industries.

The ZEEKR sales network comprises four main components: ZEEKR Centers, ZEEKR Spaces, ZEEKR Delivery Centers, and ZEEKR Houses. By the end of 2023, ZEEKR Centers, which are upscale showrooms ranging from 300 to 600 square meters, numbered 24 in China and two internationally. ZEEKR Spaces, smaller venues of 100 to 300 square meters, total 240 in China, including 42 flexible Pop-Up locations aimed at increasing customer outreach. The ZEEKR Delivery Centers, totaling 31, are strategically located outside urban areas to facilitate efficient vehicle storage and delivery. Additionally, 45 ZEEKR Houses leverage the Geely Group's service network to enhance customer experiences, situated near car dealerships across various city tiers.

ZEEKR's facilities are designed to promote community engagement and strengthen brand reputation. The ZEEKR APP enables potential customers to order vehicles with non-refundable deposits, offering various configuration options due to robust supply chain management from Geely Group. Customers have a 72-hour Configuration Confirmation Period to finalize vehicle details after placing an order, with delivery specialists assisting in pre-delivery arrangements. The remaining balance is paid upon vehicle delivery, ensuring a streamlined purchasing process.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an innovative and rapidly expanding battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company committed to transforming the automobile industry through electrification and advanced technology. With a focus on next-generation premium BEVs, ZEEKR leverages strong in-house research and development capabilities, a flexible organizational structure, and a deep understanding of customer needs to efficiently develop and launch products. The company has established a prominent position in the premium BEV market with models such as the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan, all emphasizing stylish design, high performance, and exceptional user experiences.

Since its inception in October 2021, ZEEKR has achieved remarkable milestones, including the rapid delivery of 196,633 vehicles by December 2023, with 192,441 units sold in China alone. The ZEEKR 001, recognized as the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, has been particularly successful, reaching 10,000 units delivered within four months of its launch. The company benefits from the extensive resources and industry experience of its parent company, Geely Group, which enhances ZEEKR's product innovation and operational efficiency.

ZEEKR's technological advancements are supported by its proprietary SEA platform, enabling a diverse range of vehicle types and facilitating the integration of cutting-edge technologies. The company is also focused on enhancing the customer experience through a direct-to-consumer sales model and a comprehensive network of customer touchpoints across China. Additionally, ZEEKR is expanding its charging infrastructure and forming strategic partnerships with leading firms to support its growth and global expansion.

The BEV market is experiencing significant growth, particularly in China, where sales are projected to increase dramatically in the coming years. ZEEKR aims to capitalize on this trend by expanding its vehicle portfolio and exploring new markets, including the robotaxi sector in the United States. With a strong management team and a commitment to customer-centric operations, ZEEKR is well-positioned to lead the future of premium electric mobility.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OUR COMPETITIVE STRENGTHS, page 119 
summary: The request seeks clarification on how the company maintains its identity as an "independently-run startup-style company" despite its connections to Geely Auto and the involvement of its directors with the automotive giant. It emphasizes the need for transparency regarding the company's operational independence and decision-making processes. The company's executive officers, including Mr. Conghui An, who is the only executive officer also affiliated with Geely Auto, manage operations without direct oversight from Geely, as the other officers do not hold management roles there. Mr. An is expected to step down from his position at Geely before or upon the completion of the offering. The current board structure will be adjusted post-offering to include at least three independent directors, ensuring that those with ties to Geely represent a minority. The company operates independently in key areas like research and development, utilizing its own facilities and a direct-to-consumer sales model for its battery electric vehicles. Overall, the company asserts that its characterization as an "independently-run startup-style company" is both accurate and appropriate.

summary: Leading battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturers are enhancing user experiences by creating integrated online and offline communities through a direct-to-consumer (DTC) model. Customers enjoy a variety of services, including streamlined vehicle purchasing, financial solutions, intelligent vehicle control, and lifestyle products via dedicated apps. This focus on customer engagement fosters loyalty and brand awareness. Furthermore, by hosting offline interactive events and product promotions, companies can collect valuable customer feedback, which informs product development and manufacturing processes, ultimately helping BEV manufacturers stay competitive in the market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) companies brought direct customer engagement and improved after-sale user experience to the automobile industry. Through Lotus Technology's direct customer engagement after vehicle sales, Lotus Technology is able to identify areas for improvement swiftly and provide regular updates on the key functionalities of its BEVs, leveraging its strong FOTA technologies and capabilities throughout the product lifecycle. This helps Lotus Technology identify and meet evolving customer demands. Lotus Technology has embedded ample room for future upgrades given the flexibility of its SEA and advanced hardware, such as Mobileye Q5H chips. With such continuous upgrades, Lotus Technology aims to create an enduring intelligent BEV experience for its customers. This self-owned network gives Lotus Technology direct control over the sales and marketing process and allows Lotus Technology to achieve transparent pricing and sales terms, high efficiency in communicating with users, and delivering services compared with incumbent automakers’ dealership models in China. By leveraging Lotus Technology's existing local resources, Lotus Technology is also in the process of establishing additional ZEEKR Houses in the dealership shop area to maximize the cluster effect and provide Lotus Technology's customers with a superior one-stop experience. In addition, as a key component of Lotus Technology's customer service, Lotus Technology offers a wide range of after-sales services and value-added services, such as ZEEKR Care and ZEEKR Carefree Packages.

metadata.title_summary:
title: You may face difficulties in protecting your interests, and your ability to protect your rights through U.S. courts may be limited, because we are incorporated under Cayman Islands law. 
summary: The company is an exempted entity limited by shares under Cayman Islands law, governed by its memorandum and articles of association, the revised Companies Act, and applicable common law. Shareholder rights and directors' fiduciary duties are primarily dictated by common law, which is shaped by limited local precedents and influenced by English common law, although English court rulings are merely persuasive. In contrast to jurisdictions like Delaware in the U.S., the legal framework in the Cayman Islands is less defined, particularly concerning securities laws and corporate governance. Notably, shareholders of Cayman Islands companies do not possess general rights to inspect corporate records or obtain shareholder lists, as access is at the discretion of the directors. This limitation may impede shareholders' ability to gather essential information for decision-making or proxy contests. Consequently, public shareholders may encounter more significant challenges in protecting their interests against management, the board, or controlling shareholders compared to their U.S. counterparts. The differences in corporate governance and shareholder protections between the Cayman Islands and U.S. laws are further elaborated in the section titled "Description of Share Capital — Differences in Corporate Law."

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established in the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021, is dedicated to the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles (EVs) and batteries, alongside providing automotive-related research and development services. The company emerged from a restructuring of operations previously managed by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., with the aim of creating a focused battery electric vehicle (BEV) business to attract investment and facilitate overseas listings.

The ZEEKR Group comprises several entities, each with specific roles in the EV ecosystem. Key entities include ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., an investment holding company; ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., which handles EV production and commercialization; Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., focused on electric powertrains and battery packs; and Zeekr Technology Europe AB in Sweden, specializing in research and development.

The restructuring process involved transferring relevant businesses to ZEEKR, which was recognized as a holding company for BEV operations previously under Geely Holding. In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and acquired ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay from Geely Holding for RMB485.3 million, followed by the acquisition of ZEEKR Shanghai for RMB980.4 million in August. These transactions, treated as restructuring under common control, aimed to streamline operations and enhance ZEEKR's strategic focus on advancing electric vehicle commercialization and supporting related activities.

- Table Summary: This table compares the statutory income tax rate with the effective income tax rate for the years 2021, 2022, and 2023. It highlights various adjustments such as non-deductible expenses, additional tax deductions, non-taxable income, preferential tax rates, the impact of different tax jurisdictions, changes in valuation allowance, and other factors that account for the differences between the statutory and effective rates.  
- Context: Lotus Technology evaluates subsidiaries within differing tax jurisdictions, with a specific focus on the effective versus statutory tax rates over the specified period. Key factors influencing the effective tax rate include non-deductible expenses and benefits from preferential tax regimes.  
- Special Notes: The statutory income tax rate is consistently 25.0% across all years.  

[Row Level]  
Row 1: The statutory income tax rate for 2021, 2022, and 2023 is 25.0%.  
Row 2: Non-deductible expenses reduce the rate by 1.0% in 2021, 0.8% in 2022, and 0.3% in 2023.  
Row 3: An additional tax deduction for qualified research and development expenses results in an increase of 0.6% in 2021, 0.9% in 2022, and 1.7% in 2023.  
Row 4: Non-taxable income results in no change (0.0%) to the tax rate in 2021 and 2022, but reduces it by 0.4% in 2023.  
Row 5: The tax effect of preferential tax rates decreases the rate by 0.9% in 2021, increases it by 0.8% in 2022, and decreases it by 0.3% in 2023.  
Row 6: The effect on tax rates in different tax jurisdictions is to increase the rate by 0.1% in 2021 and 2022, and to decrease it by 0.6% in 2023.  
Row 7: Changes in valuation allowance significantly decrease the rate by 23.3% in 2021, 27.8% in 2022, and 27.2% in 2023.  
Row 8: Other factors have no effect on the rate in 2021, increase it by 0.1% in 2022, and increase it by 0.5% in 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 20. Income Tax (Continued) 
summary: The summary provides an overview of tax policies and financial performance related to income tax for a group of companies operating in both the People's Republic of China (PRC) and Sweden. 

In the PRC, since 2018, the State Tax Bureau has allowed enterprises engaged in research and development (R&D) to claim a tax deduction of 175% of their R&D expenses, which increased to 200% for manufacturing enterprises in 2021 and was extended to all enterprises (except those in negative-listed industries) in 2023. The statutory income tax rate remains at 25%, with various adjustments influencing the effective tax rate, including non-deductible expenses, additional tax deductions for R&D, and the impact of different tax jurisdictions. For the years 2021 to 2023, the group experienced fluctuations in current and deferred income tax expenses, with notable benefits and expenses recorded.

In Sweden, the subsidiaries are subject to a consistent income tax rate of 20.6% for the same years. The financial data indicates a significant increase in deferred tax assets, particularly due to net operating loss carry-forwards and development expenses, alongside growing valuation allowances reflecting management's assessment of realizability.

The analysis also highlights the implications of a tax holiday, which positively impacted financial performance in 2021 but resulted in a negative effect in 2022. The group has not reported significant uncertain tax positions and expects stability in unrecognized tax benefits in the near future.

Overall, the summary illustrates the complexities of tax regulations across jurisdictions, the impact of R&D incentives, and the financial implications of tax strategies on the group's performance.
